tasks.description: Create env-driven config scaffolding and typed error base classes.
acceptance_criteria: Settings load from environment with explicit defaults.
Typed error hierarchy exists for config and exchange failures.
Tests cover missing required env and default loading.

tasks.description: Create frozen dataclasses and enums in core/types.py for the reducer and exchange interfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Core state/value models are frozen dataclasses.
Shared enums and type aliases live in core/types.py.
Tests cover immutability for representative dataclasses.

tasks.description: Implement a pure reducer skeleton in core/state_machine.py.
acceptance_criteria: Reducer has no I/O and returns (state, actions).
Reducer handles an initial no-op transition deterministically.
Tests prove the reducer is pure on repeated identical inputs.

tasks.description: Implement AP Stats normality gate utilities and initial validation tests.
acceptance_criteria: normality.py exposes a reusable normality gate function.
Outputs are deterministic for known sample datasets.
Tests cover pass and fail cases.

tasks.description: Create exchange/client.py with Starter-tier rate limiting scaffolding and shared symbol normalization hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Kraken client skeleton exists in exchange/client.py.
Starter-tier rate limiting rules are encoded in the client layer.
Tests cover representative rate limit and symbol normalization cases.

tasks.description: Implement OrderGate scaffolding with cl_ord_id generation and a circuit breaker shell.
acceptance_criteria: OrderGate is the only module that constructs order payloads.
Every emitted order payload includes cl_ord_id.
Circuit breaker shell blocks mutations after repeated failures.
Tests cover cl_ord_id generation and breaker trip behavior.

tasks.description: Create persistence/supabase.py with offline queue scaffolding for deferred writes.
acceptance_criteria: Supabase client interface exists without embedding secrets.
Offline queue behavior lives in persistence/supabase.py.
Tests cover enqueue, dequeue, and replay-ready serialization.
